response.justification: The candidate is a strong, experienced generalist engineer (Google, Meta, Dunnhumby) with excellent fundamentals in testing, CI/CD, and backend systems, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project — far short of 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He also lacks SQL Server/Oracle depth (MySQL/MongoDB instead) and has no Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; RIDE Capital was retail-investment fintech, not investment banking. The profile is a mismatch for this specialized role despite the candidate's overall seniority.
strengths: Strong overall engineering seniority with top-tier employers (Google, Meta)
Solid software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, low-latency systems experience
Excellent communication skills demonstrated through workshops, talks, and courses
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months professionally, in 2014–2015) and not Windows-platform focused — core mandatory skill unmet
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience listed; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and communication
